summary: Finished matching the HSD archive external relocation helper by replacing the inline assembly version of HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern with matching C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/archive.c. The key fix was treating archive->data relocation offsets as byte offsets, reading and writing through `(u32)archive->data + offset`, and writing the resolved address at each chained relocation site instead of accidentally writing to the start of archive->data.
what_changed: Removed the NON_MATCHING/asm split around HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern and kept the C implementation.
Moved `#pragma pop` from before HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern to after it, making the function part of the matching pragma scope.
Declared `u32 next;` before the relocation loop rather than inside the loop.
Changed relocation chain traversal from `*(u32*)((u32*)archive->data)[offset]` to `*(u32*)((u32)archive->data + offset)`, using byte-offset addressing.
Changed the relocation write from `*(u32*)archive->data = (u32)addr` to `*(u32*)((u32)archive->data + offset) = (u32)addr`, updating the current relocation slot.
Deleted the hand-written PowerPC assembly implementation for HSD_ArchiveLocateExtern.
smart_moves: Recognized that the archive relocation `offset` is compared against `archive->header.data_size`, so it should be applied as a byte offset into `archive->data`, not as an index into a `u32*` array.
Preserved the linked-list relocation pattern: read the next offset from the current relocation slot, write the resolved external address into that same slot, then advance to the saved next offset.
Kept the sentinel logic using `offset != -1` while also bounding traversal by `archive->header.data_size`, mirroring the assembly's termination behavior.
Removed a large inline asm block after achieving a matching C expression, improving maintainability without broad file churn.
decomp_lessons: When reconstructing archive relocation code, check whether stored offsets are byte offsets or typed-array indices; casting to `u32*` too early can introduce unwanted scaling.
For relocation chains, save the next pointer before overwriting the current slot with the resolved address.
If assembly shows `add base, offset` followed by `lwz/stw 0(base+offset)`, the C should generally use byte-wise address arithmetic such as `(u32)base + offset` before casting to the access type.
A small expression-level pointer arithmetic change can be enough to eliminate an entire NON_MATCHING inline assembly fallback.
naming_conventions: Used simple local names matching the relocation-chain role: `offset`, `next`, `symbols`, and `addr`.
No new project-wide naming convention was introduced by this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched PowerPC byte-offset address calculation by casting `archive->data` to an integer address before adding `offset`.
Hoisted `next` out of the while loop, likely to better match compiler register allocation or declaration ordering.
Replaced an inline asm block labeled around addresses 80380434-803804F4 with C that preserves the same control flow: symbol lookup loop, sentinel check, relocation chain traversal, bounds check.
The fix specifically avoided `((u32*)archive->data)[offset]`, which would multiply the offset by four and fail to correspond to assembly using raw byte addition.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other archive relocation functions that cast data buffers to typed pointers before applying file offsets.
Search for remaining NON_MATCHING blocks in src/sysdolphin/baselib related to HSD archive loading or relocation.
Compare other uses of `archive->header.data_size` to confirm offsets are consistently treated as byte offsets.
Look for relocation loops that overwrite a slot before saving the next chained offset.
